-----Original Message-----
From: "J. Epstein" jeeproject@yahoo.com
Date: Wed, 5 Jul 2006 04:43:27
To:LVJET@aol.com
Subject: Re: Bell 430

today , let's buy JAck goldberger a bmw 6
convertible.. get me best deal on fully loaded,,,
tell
me color combo,, he's in palm beach, Try to tell the
dealer we would like him for the first two days to
be
able to change colors., so deliver before title,if
possible.

--- LVJET@aol.com wrote:

I spoke to Paul Schrader who is in contact with
listing broker for Bell, Paul
has confided with me on the deal, not to get Paul
in
trouble with
Bell..,,Strohle and his mechanic have inspected
the
helicopter, there are several service
bulletin that need to be repaired at owners
expense.
in the $40,000 range,
same potemtiamneter problem we had 2 years ago.

Ben and Morris are so upset with Bell they
canceled
thier 3 orders for new
helicopters this morning. according to Paul he
does
not know who Jeffrey Sopher
is,,,the owner according to bell is Dick
Foster...I
would like to wait till
the smoke steeltes before jumping in to deal.
Bell knows to contact me if we are able to make a
real offer. there are some
maintenace issues that need to be addressed and
put
dollare values on.
I still believe Sthrole will take the 430. since
Bell has an exclusive on
brokering this machine.
LV

subject: Re: Bell 430
body:
lets go ahead... did we hear any more about the c++

--- lvjet@aol.com wrote:

> Ghislaine and Jeffrey,
> Rite Aid is original owner.
> I spoke to Paul Schrader, he knows the machine and
> he gives his approval.
> This B430 is not officially on the market and may
> already be sold at 3.9million. Inside deal.
> under priced, Rite Aid is in financial trouble. Paul
> said he would have asked 4.2M at least.
> I still recommend for you and Jeffrey to ride in
> this Bell 430 before making an offer, or at least
> make the sale contingent on approved test flight.
> your thoughts?
> Larry V
>  
> 1998 Bell 430 Helicopter
>  
> Total time airframe: 2,180 hours                    
>                                Serial Number: 41
>  
>             Engine #1: 2,138.9 hours    Cycles:
> 2,741
>  
>             Engine#2: 2,032.8 hours    Cycles: 2,530
>  
> Features
>  
> New paint in February 2005
> Corporate 6 seat configuration with custom ISO Quiet
> interior
> Retractable landing gear
> Certified Single-Pilot IFR
> Dual Flight Controls
> AFCS with Flight Director
> Two (2) King VHF communications radios
> Two (2) King Navigation radios
> Two (2) Mode S Transponders
> Two (2) GPS receivers
> King KLN 90B IFR certified
> Garmin 530
> DME
> ELT
> B.F. Goodrich traffic advisory system
> Argus 7000 moving map
> Radar Altimeter
> RDR 2100 color weather radar
> Stand-by attitude indicator
> Co-pilot wheel brakes
> Environmental Control system
> Aux Fuel tank 48 gallons
> Heated windshield
> Snow baffles
> Air Show passenger system
> Prerecorded passenger briefings
> Alpine stereo with 10 disc CD changer
>  
> Additional Items included in sale
>  
> Main rotor/tail rotor tie downs and engine inlet
> covers
> Rads computer track and balance system
> Spare parts inventory and tools ? seals,
> consumables, and o-rings
> Spare tail rotor blade
> Marvel tail rotor balancing kit
> Starter generator
> Engine stand
>  
> Price:   $3,900,000 US  with fresh annual inspection
>

--- lvjet@aol.com wrote:

Ghislaine and Jeffrey,
Rite Aid is original owner.
I spoke to Paul Schrader, he knows the machine and
he gives his approval.
This B430 is not officially on the market and may
already be sold at 3.9million. Inside deal.
under priced, Rite Aid is in financial trouble. Paul
said he would have asked 4.2M at least.
I still recommend for you and Jeffrey to ride in
this Bell 430 before making an offer, or at least
make the sale contingent on approved test flight.
your thoughts?
Larry V
